text.en_4: You most likely have gastroenteritis, a stomach and intestine infection that can cause vomiting or diarrhea.
text.en_6: You most likely have gastroenteritis, an infection or irritation of the stomach and intestines that can cause vomiting or diarrhea.
text.en_hl1: You likely have a stomach virus. It can cause vomiting or diarrhea.

review.notes: Reviewed for gastroenteritis_stable_hydrating v1. Patient-facing text is locally authored from source-supported concepts.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports oral rehydration, gradual diet advancement, hygiene, and return precautions for dehydration, blood in stool, fever, and severe abdominal pain.

text.en_4: You were stable enough to go home today. You can keep working on drinking fluids at home.
text.en_6: You were stable enough for discharge today. You can continue oral hydration at home.
text.en_hl1: You were safe to go home today. Keep drinking fluids.

review.notes: Reviewed for gastroenteritis_stable_hydrating v1. Patient-facing text is locally authored from source-supported concepts.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports oral rehydration, gradual diet advancement, hygiene, and return precautions for dehydration, blood in stool, fever, and severe abdominal pain.

text.en_4: Take small sips often. Water, oral rehydration solution, broth, or a sports drink can help replace fluid.
text.en_6: Take small sips often. Water, oral rehydration solution, broth, or a sports drink can help replace fluids.
text.en_hl1: Take small sips often. Use water, broth, or rehydration drink.

review.notes: Reviewed for gastroenteritis_stable_hydrating v1. Patient-facing text is locally authored from source-supported concepts.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports oral rehydration, gradual diet advancement, hygiene, and return precautions for dehydration, blood in stool, fever, and severe abdominal pain.

text.en_4: When you feel ready, start with bland foods. Wash your hands well because stomach infections can spread.
text.en_6: When you feel ready, restart bland foods and advance slowly. Wash your hands well because stomach infections can spread.
text.en_hl1: Eat bland foods when ready. Wash your hands well.

review.notes: Reviewed for gastroenteritis_stable_hydrating v1. Patient-facing text is locally authored from source-supported concepts.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports oral rehydration, gradual diet advancement, hygiene, and return precautions for dehydration, blood in stool, fever, and severe abdominal pain.

text.en_4: If you were prescribed nausea medicine, take it only as directed so you can keep fluids down.
text.en_6: If you were prescribed nausea medicine, take it only as directed so you can keep fluids down.
text.en_hl1: If you got nausea medicine, take it as told so you can drink.

review.notes: Reviewed for gastroenteritis_stable_hydrating v1. Patient-facing text is locally authored from source-supported concepts.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports oral rehydration, gradual diet advancement, hygiene, and return precautions for dehydration, blood in stool, fever, and severe abdominal pain.

text.en_4: Do not use diarrhea-stopping medicine if you have bloody stool, high fever, or severe belly pain unless a doctor tells you to.
text.en_6: Do not use diarrhea-stopping medicine if you have bloody stool, high fever, or severe abdominal pain unless a doctor tells you to.
text.en_hl1: Do not use diarrhea-stopping medicine with blood, high fever, or bad belly pain unless told.

review.notes: Reviewed for gastroenteritis_stable_hydrating v1. Patient-facing text is locally authored from source-supported concepts.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports oral rehydration, gradual diet advancement, hygiene, and return precautions for dehydration, blood in stool, fever, and severe abdominal pain.

text.en_4: Come back now for severe belly pain, bloody or black stool, fainting, confusion, or signs of dehydration.
text.en_6: Come back to the emergency department right away for severe abdominal pain, bloody or black stool, fainting, confusion, or signs of dehydration.
text.en_hl1: Come back now for bad belly pain, blood in stool, black stool, fainting, confusion, or dehydration.

review.notes: Reviewed for gastroenteritis_stable_hydrating v1. Patient-facing text is locally authored from source-supported concepts.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports oral rehydration, gradual diet advancement, hygiene, and return precautions for dehydration, blood in stool, fever, and severe abdominal pain.

text.en_4: Come back if you cannot keep fluids down, stop peeing normally, fever gets high, or symptoms keep getting worse.
text.en_6: Come back if you cannot keep fluids down, are urinating much less than usual, fever becomes high, or symptoms keep getting worse.
text.en_hl1: Come back if you cannot drink, pee much less, have high fever, or keep getting worse.

review.notes: Reviewed for gastroenteritis_stable_hydrating v1. Patient-facing text is locally authored from source-supported concepts.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports oral rehydration, gradual diet advancement, hygiene, and return precautions for dehydration, blood in stool, fever, and severe abdominal pain.

text.en_4: See your regular doctor, clinic, or urgent care in 1 to 3 days if you are not getting better.
text.en_6: See your regular doctor, clinic, or urgent care in 1 to 3 days if symptoms are not improving.
text.en_hl1: See a doctor in 1 to 3 days if you do not feel better.

review.notes: Reviewed for gastroenteritis_stable_hydrating v1. Patient-facing text is locally authored from source-supported concepts.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports oral rehydration, gradual diet advancement, hygiene, and return precautions for dehydration, blood in stool, fever, and severe abdominal pain.
